generatedSentence: Agnes, a frumpy woman in her late 50s, had lovingly maintained her sheltered cabin by a pine grove. Her pet goldfish, upscaled to an enormous size and named 'Gorilla,' seemed to activate a sense of joyful nostalgia. She spent her days fishing, growing lettuce, and occasionally watching her chicken roam, a simple life after a past she felt had cheated her. She despised the old aristocracy of the commonwealth, a prejudiced system she hoped was gone forever. In a nutshell, her life now was an unkillable peace, though sometimes her old kidney injury inched at her. She kept her antique broadsword above the fireplace, its crusty hilt a silent shield against any lingering fear, a relic from her past as a narcotic smuggler. One day, a jerky obloquy on the local newspaper told a story of an ancient evil downstream, claiming it would tanked the entire region's success. The news lurched Agnes's calm existence. A mysterious envelope containing a clue written on greywacke paper, hinting at a naughty past with the old kings, appeared on her knocker. It was an unusual invitation to a forum in the northeast. She felt herself being pecked at, forced back into action. She realized the hidden message in the clue revealed a radius of danger. She needed to be armed again, her old saber polished. Her old rival, an ancient evil sorceress, was somehow involved. She needed to start braking her usual routine and prepare, hardening her buttock muscles for the journey. Agnes, a truly admirable figure, knew this was her final action. The witchy sorceress, her wrinkly face contorted, had outlived many, and her dark magic crackled. Agnes, though often homebound, knows how to fight. Her impactful moves, a draft of her old mercenary training, were still honed. She prepared for the climactic battle against this newcomer. Her spirit, likewise unyielding, was fueled by memories of a simple vintage hockey game she’d once seen, a moment of almost holy joy. She recalled the time she'd traded nickels for her first hocket stick. This was her grandest project, a final display of her will, the owner of her own fate. She wasn’t going on any more 'forced' honeymoon trips. In an instant, the sorceress was vanquished, her defeat causing a downgrade in the region's dark aura. Agnes, a weary but victorious cyborg (revealing her true nature), felt the influx of peace, though the battle left her cadaverous and mucked with grime. The remaining workers of the old regime, led by a whacky jailor, were finally brought to justice. Her cerebral circuits, once irking, now felt calm. The system, once centric to evil, crashed. No more paperwork of oppression. She had used her dagger, imbued with ochre light from the heliacal dawn, to unite the scattered batteries of power against the foe. Even the tiny lizards on the square felt the new positive energy. The once attacked land was safe, and the myth of the aardvark (a common local legend) could finally mean peace. Agnes had become a magically potent force, a true victim of circumstance no longer.
panelSentences: Agnes, a frumpy woman in her late 50s, had lovingly maintained her sheltered cabin by a pine grove. Her pet goldfish, upscaled to an enormous size and named 'Gorilla,' seemed to activate a sense of joyful nostalgia. She spent her days fishing, growing lettuce, and occasionally watching her chicken roam, a simple life after a past she felt had cheated her. She despised the old aristocracy of the commonwealth, a prejudiced system she hoped was gone forever. In a nutshell, her life now was an unkillable peace, though sometimes her old kidney injury inched at her. She kept her antique broadsword above the fireplace, its crusty hilt a silent shield against any lingering fear, a relic from her past as a narcotic smuggler.

One day, a jerky obloquy on the local newspaper told a story of an ancient evil downstream, claiming it would tanked the entire region's success. The news lurched Agnes's calm existence. A mysterious envelope containing a clue written on greywacke paper, hinting at a naughty past with the old kings, appeared on her knocker. It was an unusual invitation to a forum in the northeast. She felt herself being pecked at, forced back into action. She realized the hidden message in the clue revealed a radius of danger. She needed to be armed again, her old saber polished. Her old rival, an ancient evil sorceress, was somehow involved. She needed to start braking her usual routine and prepare, hardening her buttock muscles for the journey.

Agnes, a truly admirable figure, knew this was her final action. The witchy sorceress, her wrinkly face contorted, had outlived many, and her dark magic crackled. Agnes, though often homebound, knows how to fight. Her impactful moves, a draft of her old mercenary training, were still honed. She prepared for the climactic battle against this newcomer. Her spirit, likewise unyielding, was fueled by memories of a simple vintage hockey game she’d once seen, a moment of almost holy joy. She recalled the time she'd traded nickels for her first hocket stick. This was her grandest project, a final display of her will, the owner of her own fate. She wasn’t going on any more 'forced' honeymoon trips.

In an instant, the sorceress was vanquished, her defeat causing a downgrade in the region's dark aura. Agnes, a weary but victorious cyborg (revealing her true nature), felt the influx of peace, though the battle left her cadaverous and mucked with grime. The remaining workers of the old regime, led by a whacky jailor, were finally brought to justice. Her cerebral circuits, once irking, now felt calm. The system, once centric to evil, crashed. No more paperwork of oppression. She had used her dagger, imbued with ochre light from the heliacal dawn, to unite the scattered batteries of power against the foe. Even the tiny lizards on the square felt the new positive energy. The once attacked land was safe, and the myth of the aardvark (a common local legend) could finally mean peace. Agnes had become a magically potent force, a true victim of circumstance no longer.
rawPanelSentences: Agnes, a **frumpy** woman in her late 50s, had **lovingly** maintained her **sheltered** cabin by a **pine** grove. Her pet **goldfish**, **upscaled** to an enormous size and named 'Gorilla,' seemed to **activate** a sense of **joyful** nostalgia. She spent her days **fishing**, growing **lettuce**, and occasionally watching her **chicken** roam, a simple life after a past she felt had **cheated** her. She despised the old **aristocracy** of the **commonwealth**, a **prejudiced** system she hoped was gone **forever**. In a **nutshell**, her life now was an **unkillable** peace, though sometimes her old **kidney** injury **inched** at her. She kept her antique **broadsword** above the fireplace, its **crusty** hilt a silent **shield** against any lingering fear, a relic from her past as a **narcotic** smuggler.

One day, a **jerky** **obloquy** on the local **newspaper** told a **story** of an ancient evil **downstream**, claiming it would **tanked** the entire region's **success**. The news **lurched** Agnes's calm existence. A mysterious **envelope** containing a clue written on **greywacke** paper, hinting at a **naughty** past with the old **kings**, appeared on her **knocker**. It was an **unusual** invitation to a **forum** in the **northeast**. She felt **herself** being **pecked** at, forced back into **action**. She realized the hidden message in the clue revealed a **radius** of danger. She needed to be **armed** again, her old **saber** polished. Her old rival, an ancient **evil** sorceress, was somehow involved. She needed to start **braking** her usual routine and prepare, hardening her **buttock** muscles for the journey.

Agnes, a truly **admirable** figure, knew this was her final **action**. The **witchy** sorceress, her **wrinkly** face contorted, had **outlived** many, and her dark magic **crackled**. Agnes, though often **homebound**, **knows** **how** to fight. Her **impactful** moves, a **draft** of her old **mercenary** training, were still honed. She prepared for the climactic battle against this **newcomer**. Her spirit, **likewise** unyielding, was fueled by memories of a simple **vintage** **hockey** game she’d once seen, a moment of almost **holy** joy. She recalled the time she'd traded **nickels** for her first **hocket** stick. This was her grandest **project**, a final **display** of her will, the **owner** of her own fate. She wasn’t going on any more 'forced' **honeymoon** trips.

In an **instant**, the sorceress was vanquished, her defeat causing a **downgrade** in the region's dark aura. Agnes, a weary but victorious **cyborg** (revealing her true nature), felt the **influx** of peace, though the battle left her **cadaverous** and **mucked** with grime. The remaining **workers** of the old regime, led by a **whacky** **jailor**, were finally brought to justice. Her **cerebral** circuits, once **irking**, now felt calm. The system, once **centric** to **evil**, **crashed**. No more **paperwork** of oppression. She had used her **dagger**, imbued with **ochre** light from the **heliacal** dawn, to **unite** the scattered **batteries** of power against the foe. Even the tiny **lizards** on the **square** felt the new positive energy. The once **attacked** land was safe, and the myth of the **aardvark** (a common local legend) could finally mean peace. Agnes had become a **magically** potent force, a true **victim** of circumstance no longer.
imagePrompt: A professional 4-panel comic book page, 2x2 grid, sequential art, dark fantasy aesthetic, gritty realism with vibrant colors, cel-shading, bold ink outlines, cinematic lighting, thick white gutters, clean black borders. An aging woman in her late 50s named Agnes, with a severe, determined expression, a thin white scar across her right eye, and short, greying hair cropped close to her head. TOP-LEFT PANEL: Agnes, looking a bit disheveled and with visible wrinkles, calmly fishing by a serene pine lake, her enormous goldfish 'Gorilla' swimming nearby. Wide shot, establishing her rustic cabin in the background. TOP-RIGHT PANEL: Agnes, with a worried frown and a mature, thoughtful expression, intently examining an unusual, ancient-looking envelope on her rough wooden cabin door knocker. Medium close-up, focusing on her hand and the envelope. BOTTOM-LEFT PANEL: Agnes, now in armored attire and wielding a glowing saber, engaged in an intense, dynamic battle with a menacing, witchy adult sorceress whose dark magic crackles around her. Dynamic action shot, low angle, showing their struggle, both characters clearly adult with mature features. BOTTOM-RIGHT PANEL: Agnes, looking exhausted but resolute, standing amidst a recovering landscape where strange lizard-like creatures scatter from a central square. Behind her, a defeated, cadaverous adult jailor with a beard is being apprehended. Full shot, showing the heliacal dawn breaking in the background.

definitions.definition: To encourage development or induce increased activity; to stimulate.
To put a device, mechanism (alarm etc.) or system into action or motion; to trigger, to actuate, to set off, to enable.
To render more reactive; excite.
To render a molecule reactive, active, or effective in performing its function.
To render a substance radioactive.
To hasten a chemical reaction, especially by heating.
To remove the limitations of demoware by providing a license; to unlock.
To aerate in order to aid decomposition of organic matter.
To organize or create a military unit or station.
To bring a player back after an injury.
A type of small fish, Carassius auratus, typically orange-colored.
definitions.example: For the children's fair we'll have a few games they can play, so that each child "wins" a goldfish as a prize.

definitions.definition: To increase in size, to scale up.
That has been scaled up.
The well-being of a community.
The entirety of a (secular) society, a polity, a state.
Republic. Often capitalized, as Commonwealth.
A domestic fowl, Gallus gallus, especially when young.
The meat from this bird eaten as food.
The young of any bird; a chick.
A coward.
A young or inexperienced person.
A young, attractive, slim man, usually having little body hair; compare chickenhawk.
The game of dare.
A simple dance in which the movements of a chicken are imitated.
Cowardly.
definitions.example: Why do you refuse to fight? Huh, I guess you're just too chicken.

definitions.definition: A tramp or homeless young person with poor cleanliness.
Dried eye mucus.
A member of an urban subculture with roots in punk and grebo, characterized by antiestablishment attitudes and an unkempt appearance.
Having a crust, especially a thick one.
(of a person or behavior) Short-tempered and gruff but, sometimes, with a harmless or benign inner nature.
Of very low quality.
An edible plant, Lactuca sativa and its close relatives, having a head of green and/or purple leaves.
The leaves of the lettuce plant, eaten as a vegetable; as a dish often mixed with other ingredients, dressing etc.
definitions.example: I’ll have a ham sandwich with lettuce and tomato.

definitions.definition: United States paper currency; dollars.
definitions.example: Twelve dollars an hour? That's a lot of lettuce!

definitions.definition: A strong yellow-green colour, like that of lettuce (also called lettuce green).
The shell that surrounds the kernel of a nut.
A short book summarizing an area of law.
To summarize (from the term in a nutshell).

Any substance or drug that reduces pain, induces sleep and may alter mood or behaviour; in some contexts, especially in reference to the opiates-and-opioids class, especially in reference to illegal drugs, and often both.
Any type of numbing drug.
Of, or relating to narcotics.
(pharmaceutical effect) Inducing sleep; causing narcosis.
To provide cover from damage or harassment; to shield; to protect.
To take cover.
definitions.example: During the rainstorm, we sheltered under a tree.

definitions.definition: Protected, as from wind or weather.
definitions.example: The boat was much safer, during the storm, in the sheltered cove.

definitions.definition: (Of a person) who grew up being overprotected by parents or other guardians; often implies a lack of social skills, worldly experience, etc.
Anything that protects or defends; defense; shelter; protection.
A shape like that of a shield; usually, an inverted triangle with sides that curve inward to form a pointed bottom, commonly used for police identifications and company logos.
A large expanse of exposed stable Precambrian rock.
A place with a toilet seat: an outhouse; a lavatory.
Parts at the front and back of a vehicle which are meant to absorb the impact of a collision
An extremely long time.
definitions.example: I haven't seen him in forever!

definitions.definition: A mythical time in the infinite future that will never come.
definitions.example: Sure, I'd be happy to meet with you on the 12th of forever.

definitions.definition: Permanent, lasting
(duration) For all time, for all eternity; for a lifetime; for an infinite amount of time.
definitions.example: I shall love you forever.

definitions.definition: (duration) For a very long time, a seeming eternity.
definitions.example: We had to wait forever to get inside.

definitions.definition: (frequency) Constantly or frequently.
definitions.example: You are forever nagging me.

definitions.definition: The act of catching fish.
definitions.example: We had a good day's fishing at the weekend.

definitions.definition: The act of catching other forms of seafood, separately or together with fish.
Commercial fishing: the business or industry of catching fish and other seafood for sale.
definitions.example: This is good news for the fishing industry.

definitions.definition: A fishery, a place for catching fish.
Dowdy, unkempt, or unfashionable.
definitions.example: She came to the door in a frumpy housedress and bedroom slippers.

definitions.definition: Bad-tempered.
(history) A type of early modern sword that has a broad double-edged blade for cutting (as opposed to the more slender thrust-oriented rapier) and a basket hilt.
A person armed with such a sword.
Any type of sword that is comparatively long; depending on context, applied to swords of the Bronze Age, Migration period, Viking Age and Renaissance era.
A European sword with a long, straight double-edged blade, a cruciform hilt, and a grip for two-handed use; prevalent from the 14th to 16th centuries.
Any coniferous tree of the genus Pinus.
definitions.example: The northern slopes were covered mainly in pine.

definitions.definition: Any tree (usually coniferous) which resembles a member of this genus in some respect.
The wood of this tree.
(obsolete except South Africa) A pineapple.
To have a negative impact on (someone's position, chances etc.).
To cause prejudice in; to bias the mind of.
Having prejudices.
(followed by a preposition) To advance very slowly, or by a small amount (in a particular direction).
definitions.example: Fearful of falling, he inched along the window ledge.

definitions.definition: To drive by inches, or small degrees.
To deal out by inches; to give sparingly.
(in combination) Being a certain number of inches in length.
The nobility, or the hereditary ruling class.
Government by such a class, or a state with such a government
A class of people considered (not normally universally) superior to others
An organ in the body that filters the blood, producing urine.
This organ (of an animal) cooked as food.
Constitution, temperament, nature, type, character, disposition. (usually used of people)
A waiter.
The largest of the apes, native to the forests of central Africa, and known for their trait of knuckle-walking.
A big and brutish man or a thug; a goon or ruffian.
A powerful person or organization; a heavyweight or behemoth.
Not killable; unable to be killed.
Feeling or causing joy.
To violate rules in order to gain advantage from a situation.

definitions.definition: To be unfaithful to one's spouse or partner.
definitions.example: After he found out his wife cheated, he left her.

definitions.definition: To manage to avoid something even though it seemed unlikely.
definitions.example: He cheated death when his car collided with a moving train.

definitions.definition: To deceive; to fool; to trick.
definitions.example: He cheated his way into office.

definitions.definition: Having been deceived.
definitions.example: The people feel cheated by the government's decision.

definitions.definition: In a loving manner, affectionately.
To make such a sudden, unsteady movement.
To swallow or eat greedily; to devour; hence, to swallow up.
To leave someone in the lurch; to cheat.
To rob.
To evade by stooping; to lurk.
To make dark; overshadow
To deliberately make more confusing in order to conceal the truth.

definitions.definition: To alter code while preserving its behavior but concealing its structure and intent.

definitions.definition: Obfuscated; darkened; obscured.
The compass point halfway between north and east, specifically 45°, abbreviated as NE.
Towards the northeast; northeastward.
In the northeast; northeastern.
From the northeast, northeasterly.
Mischievous; tending to misbehave or act badly (especially of a child).
definitions.example: Some naughty boys at school hid the teacher's lesson notes.

definitions.definition: Sexually provocative; now in weakened sense, risqué, cheeky.
definitions.example: I bought some naughty lingerie for my honeymoon.

definitions.definition: Evil, wicked, morally reprehensible.
Bad, worthless, substandard.
A sequence of real or fictional events; or, an account of such a sequence.

definitions.definition: A lie, fiction.

definitions.definition: (usually pluralized) A soap opera.

definitions.definition: History.
A sequence of events, or a situation, such as might be related in an account.
definitions.example: I tried it again; same story, no error message, nothing happened.

definitions.definition: A chronological collection of pictures or short videos published by a user on an application or website that is typically only available for a short period.
To tell as a story; to relate or narrate about.
The achievement of one's aim or goal.
definitions.example: His third attempt to pass the entrance exam was a success.

definitions.definition: Financial profitability.
definitions.example: Don't let success go to your head.

definitions.definition: One who, or that which, achieves assumed goals.
definitions.example: Scholastically, he was a success.

definitions.definition: The fact of getting or achieving wealth, respect or fame.
definitions.example: She is country music's most recent success.

definitions.definition: Something which happens as a consequence; the outcome or result.
To bruise and crush; to knead

definitions.definition: To pulverise with a harrow
To operate (a) brake(s).
To be stopped or slowed (as if) by braking.

antonyms: put the pedal to the metal

definitions.definition: The act of applying brakes.
definitions.example: the acceleratings and brakings of a vehicle

definitions.definition: To strike or pierce with the beak or bill (of a bird).
definitions.example: The birds pecked at their food.

definitions.definition: To form by striking with the beak or a pointed instrument.
definitions.example: to peck a hole in a tree

definitions.definition: To strike, pick, thrust against, or dig into, with a pointed instrument, especially with repeated quick movements.
To seize and pick up with the beak, or as if with the beak; to bite; to eat; often with up.
To do something in small, intermittent pieces.
definitions.example: He has been pecking away at that project for some time now.

definitions.definition: To type by searching for each key individually.
To type in general.
To kiss briefly.
To throw.
To lurch forward; especially, of a horse, to stumble after hitting the ground with the toe instead of the flat of the foot.
(reflexive pronoun) Her; the female object of a verb or preposition that also appears as the subject.
definitions.example: She injured herself.

definitions.definition: She; an intensive repetition of the female subject, often used to indicate the exclusiveness of that person as the only satisfier of the predicate.
definitions.example: She was injured herself.

definitions.definition: The subject or non-reflexive object of a predicate; she (used of upper-class ladies, or sarcastically, of women who imagine themselves to be more important than others)
definitions.example: Have you seen herself yet this morning?

definitions.definition: A device, usually hinged with a striking plate, used for knocking on a door.
A person who knocks.
A critic; one who disparages.
A person who knocks (denigrates) something.
(usually in the plural) A woman's breasts.
(especially Cardigan) A dwarf, goblin, or sprite imagined to dwell in mines and to indicate the presence of ore by knocking.
A mechanical device in a pinball table that produces a loud percussive noise.
A person who is strikingly handsome or otherwise admirable; a stunner.
A large cockroach, especially Blaberus giganteus, of semitropical America, which is able to produce a loud knocking sound.
A large, boulder-shaped outcrop of bedrock in an otherwise low-lying landscape, chiefly associated with a mélange.
One who defaults on payment of a wager.
A male monarch; a man who heads a monarchy. If it's an absolute monarchy, then he is the supreme ruler of his nation.
definitions.example: Henry VIII was the king of England from 1509 to 1547.

definitions.definition: A powerful or majorly influential person.
definitions.example: Howard Stern styled himself as the "king of all media".

definitions.definition: Something that has a preeminent position.
definitions.example: In times of financial panic, cash is king.

definitions.definition: A component of certain games.
A king skin.
definitions.example: Oi mate, have you got kings?

definitions.definition: A male dragonfly; a drake.
A king-sized bed.
A male person.
To crown king, to make (a person) king.
To rule over as king.
To perform the duties of a king.
To assume or pretend preeminence (over); to lord it over.
To promote a piece of draughts/checkers that has traversed the board to the opposite side, that piece subsequently being permitted to move backwards as well as forwards.
To dress and perform as a drag king.
A drinking game that uses playing cards.
A sounding stone, a Chinese musical instrument.
To fail or fall (often used in describing the economy or the stock market); to degenerate or decline rapidly; to plummet.
To attract the attacks of an enemy target in cooperative team-based combat, so that one's teammates can defeat the enemy in question more efficiently.
To put (fuel, etc.) into a tank.
To deliberately lose a sports match with the intent of gaining a perceived future competitive advantage.
To resist damage; to be attacked without being hurt.
To contemplate a decision for a long time; to go in the tank.
To stand; to tolerate.
Drunk
A hard dark sandstone with poorly sorted angular grains of quartz, feldspar, and small rock fragments in a compact, clay-fine matrix.
Something that is unusual; an anomaly.
Not usual, out of the ordinary
definitions.example: Every once in a while, though, Idol comes across an unusual talent who just blows everyone away.

definitions.definition: Characterized by physical jerking.
Having the behavior of a jerk (unpleasant person).
To stream downward.
Lower down, in relation to a river, stream or flow of fluid
definitions.example: She lives downstream from the dam.

definitions.definition: In the direction from the server to the client.
Towards the 3' end of a DNA molecule.
(metaphoric) Influenced by something; being a consequence of something.
Following the path of a river or stream
definitions.example: We spent the day paddling downstream in our canoes.

definitions.definition: Moral badness; wickedness; malevolence; the forces or behaviors that are the opposite or enemy of good.
definitions.example: Evil lacks spirituality, hence its need for mind control.

definitions.definition: Something which impairs the happiness of a being or deprives a being of any good; something which causes suffering of any kind to sentient beings; harm; injury; mischief.
A malady or disease; especially in the phrase king's evil (scrofula).
Intending to harm; malevolent.
definitions.example: an evil plot to brainwash and even kill innocent people

definitions.definition: Morally corrupt.
definitions.example: Do you think that companies that engage in animal testing are evil?

definitions.definition: Unpleasant, foul (of odour, taste, mood, weather, etc.).
Producing or threatening sorrow, distress, injury, or calamity; unpropitious; calamitous.
Having harmful qualities; not good; worthless or deleterious.
definitions.example: an evil beast; an evil plant; an evil crop

definitions.definition: Undesirable; harmful; bad practice
definitions.example: Global variables are evil; storing processing context in object member variables allows those objects to be reused in a much more flexible way.

definitions.definition: A paper or cardboard wrapper used to enclose small, flat items, especially letters, for mailing.
Something that envelops; a wrapping.
A bag containing the lifting gas of a balloon or airship; fabric that encloses the gas-bags of an airship.
A mathematical curve, surface, or higher-dimensional object that is the tangent to a given family of lines, curves, surfaces, or higher-dimensional objects.
A curve that bounds another curve or set of curves, as the modulation envelope of an amplitude-modulated carrier wave in electronics.
The shape of a sound, which may be controlled by a synthesizer or sampler.
The information used for routing a message that is transmitted with the message but not part of its contents.
An enclosing structure or cover, such as a membrane; a space between two membranes
The set of limitations within which a technological system can perform safely and effectively.
The nebulous covering of the head or nucleus of a comet; a coma.
An earthwork in the form of a single parapet or a small rampart, sometimes raised in the ditch and sometimes beyond it.
Abusive language.
Disgrace.
A false accusation; malevolent rumors.
The long bone in the forearm, on the side of the thumb.
The lighter bone (or fused portion of bone) in the forelimb of an animal.
One of the major veins of the insect wing, between the subcosta and the media; the vein running along the costal edge of the discal cell.
A line segment between any point of a circle or sphere and its center.
The length of this line segment.
Anything resembling a radius, such as the spoke of a wheel, the movable arm of a sextant, or one of the radiating lines of a spider's web.
A publication, usually published daily or weekly and usually printed on cheap, low-quality paper, containing news and other articles.
A quantity of or one of the types of paper on which newspapers are printed.
To cover with newspaper.
definitions.example: She newspapered one end of the room before painting the bookcase.

definitions.definition: To engage in the business of journalism (usually used only in the gerund, newspapering)
definitions.example: He newspapered his way through the South on the sports beat, avoiding dry towns.

definitions.definition: To harass in newspaper articles.
definitions.example: He was newspapered out of public life.

definitions.definition: A light sword, sharp along the front edge, part of the back edge, and at the point.
A modern fencing sword modeled after the sabre.
To strike or kill with a sabre.
A place for discussion.
A gathering for the purpose of discussion.
A form of discussion involving a panel of presenters and often participation by members of the audience.
An Internet message board where users can post messages regarding one or more topics of discussion.
definitions.example: Trish was an admin on three forums, and had no trouble at all when it came to moderating them.

definitions.definition: A square or marketplace in a Roman town, used for public business and commerce.
(usually in the plural) Each of the two large fleshy halves of the posterior part of the body between the base of the back, the perineum and the top of the legs.
The convexity of a ship behind, under the stern.
To take by the arm; to take up in one's arms.
To supply with armour or (later especially) weapons.
definitions.example: The king armed his knights with swords and shields.

definitions.definition: To prepare a tool or a weapon for action; to activate.
definitions.example: Remember to arm the alarm system before leaving for work.

definitions.definition: To cover or furnish with a plate, or with whatever will add strength, force, security, or efficiency.
definitions.example: to arm the hit of a sword; to arm a hook in angling

definitions.definition: To furnish with means of defence; to prepare for resistance; to fortify, in a moral sense.
To take up weapons; to arm oneself.
To fit (a magnet) with an armature.
(sometimes in combination) Equipped, especially with a weapon.
definitions.example: nuclear-armed

definitions.definition: (of a weapon) Prepared for use; loaded.
Furnished with whatever serves to add strength, force, or efficiency.
Having prickles or thorns.
Deserving of the highest esteem or admiration.
definitions.example: It's admirable that Shelley overcame her handicap and excelled in her work.

definitions.definition: Something done so as to accomplish a purpose.
A way of motion or functioning.
definitions.example: Knead bread with a rocking action.

definitions.definition: Fast-paced activity.
definitions.example: a movie full of exciting action

definitions.definition: A mechanism; a moving part or assembly.
definitions.example: a rifle action

definitions.definition: The mechanism, that is the set of moving mechanical parts, of a keyboard instrument, like a piano, which transfers the motion of the key to the sound-making device.
The distance separating the strings and the fretboard on a guitar.
Sexual intercourse.
definitions.example: She gave him some action.

definitions.definition: Combat.
definitions.example: He saw some action in the Korean War.

definitions.definition: A charge or other process in a law court (also called lawsuit and actio).

A mapping from a pairing of mathematical objects to one of them, respecting their individual structures. The pairing is typically a Cartesian product or a tensor product. The object that is not part of the output is said to act on the other object. In any given context, action is used as an abbreviation for a more fully named notion, like group action or left group action.
The product of energy and time, especially the product of the Lagrangian and time.
The event or connected series of events, either real or imaginary, forming the subject of a play, poem, or other composition; the unfolding of the drama of events.
(painting and sculpture) The attitude or position of the several parts of the body as expressive of the sentiment or passion depicted.
Spin put on the bowling ball.
A share in the capital stock of a joint-stock company, or in the public funds.
To act on a request etc, in order to put it into effect.
To initiate a legal action against someone.
Demanding or signifying the start of something, usually an act or scene of a theatric performance.
definitions.example: The director yelled ‘Action!’ before the camera started rolling.

definitions.definition: Confined to one's home, unable to leave it for some reason.
Heading homeward, homeward bound.
The period of time immediately following a marriage.
A trip taken by a newly married couple during this period.
definitions.example: We went to Greece for our honeymoon.

definitions.definition: A period of goodwill at the beginning of a new term or relationship (e.g. towards a newly elected politician or in respect of a new business arrangement).
definitions.example: Now that the honeymoon is over, it’s time for us to get down to the business at hand.

definitions.definition: To have a honeymoon (a trip taken by a couple after wedding).
definitions.example: My parents honeymooned at Niagara falls.

definitions.definition: In medieval music, a rhythmic linear technique using the alternation of notes, pitches, or chords. A single melody is shared between two (or occasionally more) voices such that alternately one voice sounds while the other rests.
Ice hockey, a game on ice in which two teams of six players skate and try to score by shooting a puck into the opposing team's net, using their sticks.
Field hockey, a team sport played on a pitch on solid ground where players have to hit a ball into a net using a hockey stick.
A variation of hockey, such as roller hockey, street hockey, shinny, or ball hockey.
A thing that is extremely holy; used almost exclusively in Holy of Holies.
Dedicated to a religious purpose or a god.
definitions.example: I'm planning to visit the holy city of Jerusalem this Christmas.

definitions.definition: Revered in a religion.
Morally perfect or flawless, or nearly so.
definitions.example: My grandmother is a very holy woman.

definitions.definition: Separated or set apart from (something unto something or someone else).
Set apart or dedicated for a specific purpose, or for use by a single entity or person.
Used as an intensifier in various interjections.
definitions.example: Holy cow, I can’t believe he actually lost the race!

definitions.definition: The means by which something is accomplished.
definitions.example: I am not interested in the why, but in the how.

definitions.definition: To what degree.
definitions.example: How often do you practice?

definitions.definition: In what manner.
definitions.example: How do you solve this puzzle?

definitions.definition: In what state.
definitions.example: How are you?

definitions.definition: Used as a modifier to indicate surprise, delight, or other strong feelings in an exclamation.
definitions.example: How very interesting!

definitions.definition: The manner or way that.
definitions.example: I remember how I solved this puzzle.

definitions.definition: That, the fact that, the way that.
An old person.
Having wrinkles.
Willing; favourable; ready; eager.
Self-willed; willful.
Of, pertaining to, or characteristic of witches; witchlike.
definitions.example: The children are scared to go near the witchy old woman's house at night.

definitions.definition: Knowledge; the state of knowing.
To perceive the truth or factuality of; to be certain of or that.
definitions.example: He knew something terrible was going to happen.

definitions.definition: To be aware of; to be cognizant of.
definitions.example: Did you know Michelle and Jack were getting divorced? ― Yes, I knew.

definitions.definition: To be acquainted or familiar with; to have encountered.
definitions.example: I know your mother, but I’ve never met your father.

definitions.definition: To experience.
definitions.example: Their relationship knew ups and downs.

definitions.definition: To be able to distinguish, to discern, particularly by contrast or comparison; to recognize the nature of.
definitions.example: I wouldn't know one from the other.

definitions.definition: To recognize as the same (as someone or something previously encountered) after an absence or change.
To understand or have a grasp of through experience or study.
definitions.example: His mother tongue is Italian, but he also knows French and English.

definitions.definition: To have sexual relations with. This meaning normally specified in modern English as e.g. to ’know someone in the biblical sense’ or to ‘know Biblically.’
To have knowledge; to have information, be informed.
definitions.example: He knows about 19th century politics.

definitions.definition: To be or become aware or cognizant.
definitions.example: Did you know Michelle and Jack were getting divorced? ― Yes, I knew.

definitions.definition: To be acquainted (with another person).
To be able to play or perform (a song or other piece of music).
definitions.example: Do you know "Blueberry Hill"?

definitions.definition: To make a fizzing, popping sound.
definitions.example: a crackling fire

definitions.definition: Having a crackle, or glaze resembling many small cracks.
One motivated by gain, especially monetary.
A person employed to fight in an armed conflict who is not a member of the state or military group for which they are fighting and whose primary motivation is private gain.
One hired to engage in a figurative battle, as a corporate takeover, a lawsuit, or a political campaign.
Motivated by private gain.
(manner) In a similar manner.
definitions.example: Public transportation is virtually inaccessible in this country; likewise, its hospitals are also not very user-friendly.

definitions.definition: Also; moreover; too.
definitions.example: Margaret enjoys playing tennis on Saturdays, Jeremy likewise.

definitions.definition: The same to you; used as a response.
definitions.example: It was very nice meeting you, Samantha. ― Likewise, Mr Thompson.

definitions.definition: A show or spectacle.
definitions.example: The trapeze artist put on an amazing acrobatic display.

definitions.definition: A piece of work to be presented visually.
definitions.example: Pupils are expected to produce a wall display about a country of their choice.

definitions.definition: An electronic screen that shows graphics or text.
The presentation of information for visual or tactile reception.
(travel, in a reservation system) The asterisk symbol, used to denote that the following information will be displayed, eg, *H will "display history".
To show conspicuously; to exhibit; to demonstrate; to manifest.
To make a display; to act as one making a show or demonstration.
To extend the front of (a column), bringing it into line.
To make conspicuous by using large or prominent type.
To discover; to descry.
To spread out, to unfurl.
A current of air, usually coming into a room or vehicle.
Draw through a flue of gasses (smoke) resulting from a combustion process.
An act of drinking.
The quantity of liquid (such as water, alcohol, or medicine) drunk in one swallow.

definitions.definition: A dose (of medicine, alcohol, etc.)
Beer drawn from a cask or keg rather than a bottle or can.
Depth of water needed to float a ship; depth below the water line to the bottom of a vessel's hull; depth of water drawn by a vessel.
An early version of a written work (such as a book or e-mail) or drawing.
definitions.example: His first drafts were better than most authors' final products.

definitions.definition: A preliminary sketch or outline for a plan.
A cheque, an order for money to be paid.
Conscription, the system of forcing people to serve in the military.

definitions.definition: A system of forcing or convincing people to take an elected position.
A system of assigning rookie players to professional sports teams.
The pulling force (tension) on couplers and draft gear during a slack stretched condition.
The bevel given to the pattern for a casting, so that it can be drawn from the sand without damaging the mould.
(possibly obsolete) The action or an act (especially of a beast of burden or vehicle) of pulling something along or back.

definitions.definition: (possibly obsolete) The act of drawing in a net for fish.
(possibly obsolete) That which is drawn in; a catch, a haul.
definitions.example: he cast his net, which brought him a very great draft

definitions.definition: A quantity that is requisitioned or drawn out from a larger population.
To write a first version, make a preliminary sketch.
To draw in outline; to make a draught, sketch, or plan of, as in architectural and mechanical drawing.
To write a law.
To conscript a person, force a person to serve in some capacity, especially in the military.

definitions.definition: To select someone (or something) for a particular role or purpose.
definitions.example: There was a campaign to draft Smith to run for President.

definitions.definition: To select and separate an animal or animals from a group.
definitions.example: The calves were drafted from the cows.

definitions.definition: To select a rookie player onto a professional sports team.
definitions.example: After his last year of college football, he was drafted by the Miami Dolphins.

definitions.definition: To follow very closely (behind another vehicle), thereby providing an aerodynamic advantage to both lead and follower and conserving energy or increasing speed.
To draw out; to call forth.
To draw fibers out of a clump, for spinning in the production of yarn.
Referring to drinks on tap, in contrast to bottled.
definitions.example: I'd rather have a fresh, cheap draft beer.

definitions.definition: Referring to animals used for pulling heavy loads.
definitions.example: A Clydesdale is a draft horse.

definitions.definition: One who owns something.
definitions.example: The police recovered the stolen car and returned it to its owner.

definitions.definition: The captain of a ship.
To live longer than; continue to live after the death of; overlive; survive.
To live through or past (a given time).
To surpass in duration; outlast.
To live longer; continue to live.
One who has recently come to a community; a recent arrival.
A new participant in some activity; a neophyte.
A silvery elemental metal with an atomic number of 28 and symbol Ni.
A coin worth 5 cents.
(by extension) Five dollars.
(by extension) Five hundred dollars.
(sometimes the nickel or the hot nickel) Interstate 5, a highway that runs along the west coast of the United States.
A playing card with the rank of five
A five-year prison sentence.
A defensive formation with five defensive backs, one of whom is a nickelback, instead of the more common four.
To plate with nickel.
(of water) Salty or slightly salty, as a mixture of fresh and sea water, such as that found in estuaries.
Distasteful; unpleasant; not appealing to the taste.
Repulsive
The yield of grapes or wine from a vineyard or district during one season.
Wine, especially high-quality, identified as to year and vineyard or district of origin.
The harvesting of a grape crop and the initial pressing of juice for winemaking.
The year or place in which something is produced.
To harvest (grapes).
To make (wine) from grapes.
Of or relating to a vintage, or to wine identified by a specific vintage.
Having an enduring appeal; high-quality.
Classic (such as watches, video or computer games from the 1980s and early 1990s, old magazines, etc.).
A planned endeavor, usually with a specific goal and accomplished in several steps or stages.
(usually in the plural) An urban low-income housing building.
definitions.example: Projects like Pruitt-Igoe were considered irreparably dangerous and demolished.

definitions.definition: An idle scheme; an impracticable design.

definitions.definition: A raw recruit who the team hopes will improve greatly with coaching; a long shot diamond in the rough
A projectile.
A projection.
The place from which a thing projects.
To extend beyond a surface.
To cast (an image or shadow) upon a surface; to throw or cast forward; to shoot forth.
To extend (a protrusion or appendage) outward.
To make plans for; to forecast.

definitions.definition: To present (oneself), to convey a certain impression, usually in a good way.
To assume qualities or mindsets in others based on one's own personality.
To change the projection (or coordinate system) of spatial data with another projection.
To draw straight lines from a fixed point through every point of any body or figure, and let these fall upon a surface so as to form the points of a new figure.
Having impact.
A very short period of time; a moment.
definitions.example: She paused for only an instant, which was just enough time for John to change the subject.

definitions.definition: A single, usually precise, point in time.
definitions.example: The instant the alarm went off, he fled the building.

definitions.definition: A beverage or food which has been pre-processed to reduce preparation time, especially instant coffee.
A camera that uses self-developing film to create a chemically developed print shortly after the photograph is taken.
To irritate; annoy; bother
A person who performs labor for a living, especially manual labor.
A nonreproductive social insect, such as ant, bee, termite, or wasp.
A thread performing one instance of a particular task within a program.

definitions.definition: Zany; eccentric
Being in the centre; central.
Pertaining to a nerve centre.
(of diatoms) Being a member of the class Centrales
A person who is part machine, a robot who is part organic.
A robot who has an organic past.
A human with electronic or bionic prostheses.
In a magical manner; by magic, or as if by magic.
To shovel muck.

definitions.definition: To manure with muck.
To do a dirty job.
To pass, to fold without showing one's cards, often done when a better hand has already been revealed.
Any reptile of the order Squamata that is not a snake, usually having four legs, external ear openings, movable eyelids and a long slender body and tail.
(chiefly in attributive use) Lizard skin, the skin of these reptiles.
An unctuous person.
A coward.

(rock paper scissors) A hand forming a "D" shape with the tips of the thumb and index finger touching (a handshape resembling a lizard), that beats paper and Spock and loses to rock and scissors in rock-paper-scissors-lizard-Spock.
(in compounds) A person who idly spends time in a specified place, especially a promiscuous female.
definitions.example: lounge lizard; lot lizard; beach lizard; truck stop lizard

definitions.definition: A reduction of a rating, as a financial or credit rating.
A downhill gradient on a road or railway.
To place lower in position.
definitions.example: The stock was downgraded from 'buy' to 'sell'.

definitions.definition: To 'dumb down', reduce in complexity, or remove unnecessary parts.
To disparage.
To reduce the official estimate of a storm's intensity.
To revert software back to an older version.
A stabbing weapon, similar to a sword but with a short, double-edged blade.
The text character †; the obelus.
A point scored near the end of the game (clutch time) to take or increase the scorer's team lead, so that they are likely to win
definitions.example: Curry's last-minute 3-point dagger silenced the criticism for his so-called failure to come up big in big moments.

definitions.definition: To pierce with a dagger; to stab.
An earth pigment containing silica, aluminum and ferric oxide
A somewhat dark yellowish orange colour
The stop codon sequence "UAA."
Money, especially gold.
Any of various brown-coloured hesperiid butterflies of the genus Trapezites.
To cover or tint with ochre.
Having a yellow-orange colour.
Referring to cultures that covered their dead with ochre.
A polygon with four sides of equal length and four right angles; an equilateral rectangle; a regular quadrilateral.
Something characterized by a square, or nearly square, form.
An L- or T-shaped tool used to place objects or draw lines at right angles.
An open space or park, often in the center of a town, not necessarily square in shape, often containing trees, seating and other features pleasing to the eye.
The product of a number or quantity multiplied by itself; the second power of a number, value, term or expression.
definitions.example: 64 is the square of 8.

definitions.definition: A body of troops drawn up in a square formation.
(1950s slang) A socially conventional or conservative person; a person who has little or no interest in the latest fads or trends: still sometimes used in modern terminology.
definitions.example: Why do you always wear a tie? Don't be such a square!

definitions.definition: The symbol # on a telephone; hash.
definitions.example: Enter your account number followed by a square.

definitions.definition: The central area of a cricket field, with one or more pitches of which only one is used at a time.
definitions.example: An ideal playing area is roughly circular in shape with a central area, the cricket square, measuring 27.44 metres by 27.44 metres and boundaries 45.75 metres from the sides of the square.

definitions.definition: A unit of measurement of area, equal to a 10 foot by 10 foot square, i.e. 100 square feet or roughly 9.3 square metres. Used in real estate for the size of a house or its rooms, though progressively being replaced by square metres in metric countries such as Australia.
(roofing) A unit used in measuring roof area equivalent to 100 square feet (9.29 m2) of roof area.
(academia) A mortarboard
A square meal.
definitions.example: Even when times were tough, we got three squares a day.

definitions.definition: Exact proportion; justness of workmanship and conduct; regularity; rule.
The relation of harmony, or exact agreement; equality; level.
The position of planets distant ninety degrees from each other; a quadrate.
The act of squaring, or quarrelling; a quarrel.
Cigarette.
A vat used for fermentation.
To adjust so as to align with or place at a right angle to something else; in particular:
definitions.example: The casting was mounted on a milling machine so that its sides could be squared.

definitions.definition: To resolve or reconcile; to suit or fit.
definitions.example: John can square this question up for us.

definitions.definition: To adjust or adapt so as to bring into harmony with something.
definitions.example: I cannot square the results of the experiment with my hypothesis.

definitions.definition: Of a value, term or expression, to multiply by itself; to raise to the second power.
To draw, with a pair of compasses and a straightedge only, a square with the same area as.

definitions.definition: To make a short low pass sideways across the pitch
To take opposing sides; to quarrel.
To accord or agree exactly; to be consistent with; to suit; to fit.
To go to opposite sides; to take an attitude of offense or defense, or of defiance; to quarrel.
To take a boxing attitude; often with up or off.
To form with four sides and four right angles.
To form with right angles and straight lines, or flat surfaces.

definitions.definition: To compare with, or reduce to, any given measure or standard.
To hold a quartile position respecting.
Shaped like a square (the polygon).
Forming a right angle, especially at right angles with the mast or the keel, and parallel to the horizon; said of the yards of a square-rigged vessel when they are so braced.
definitions.example: a square corner

definitions.definition: Of numbers formed by multiplying two equal numbers.
definitions.example: 9 is a square number.

definitions.definition: Used in the names of units of area formed by multiplying a unit of length by itself.
definitions.example: square metre

definitions.definition: Honest; straightforward.
definitions.example: square dealing

definitions.definition: Fair.
definitions.example: I'm just looking for a square deal on my car repair.

definitions.definition: Even; tied
definitions.example: The sides were square at the end of the half.

definitions.definition: Socially conventional; boring.
In line with the batsman's popping crease.
Correctly aligned with respect to something else.
Hearty; vigorous.

definitions.definition: Having a shape broad for the height, with angular rather than curving outlines.

definitions.definition: A device used to power electric devices, consisting of a set of electrically connected electrochemical or, archaically, electrostatic cells. A single such cell when used by itself.
The infliction of unlawful physical violence on a person, legally distinguished from assault, which includes the threat of impending violence.
A coordinated group of artillery weapons.
An elevated platform on which cannon could be placed.
An array of similar things.

definitions.definition: A set of small cages where hens are kept for the purpose of farming their eggs.
The catcher and the pitcher together
Two or more major pieces on the same rank, file, or diagonal
A marching percussion ensemble; a drumline.
The state of a firearm when it is possible to be fired.
Apparatus for preparing or serving meals.
One that is harmed—killed, injured, subjected to oppression, deceived, or otherwise adversely affected—by someone or something, especially another person or event, force, or condition; in particular:

definitions.definition: A living being which is slain and offered as a sacrifice, usually in a religious rite.
Routine work involving written documents
Written documents
Excessive paperwork, busy work, red tape.
A flow inward or into something; a coming in.

definitions.definition: That which flows or comes in.
Influence; power.
To apply violent force to someone or something.

definitions.definition: To aggressively challenge a person, idea, etc., with words (particularly in newspaper headlines, because it typesets into less space than "criticize" or similar).
definitions.example: She published an article attacking the recent pay cuts.

definitions.definition: To begin to affect; to act upon injuriously or destructively; to begin to decompose or waste.
To deal with something in a direct way; to set to work upon.
definitions.example: I attacked the meal with a hearty appetite.

definitions.definition: To aim balls at the batsman’s wicket.
To set a field, or bowl in a manner designed to get wickets.
To bat aggressively, so as to score runs quickly.
To move forward in an active attempt to score a point, as opposed to trying not to concede.
To accelerate quickly in an attempt to get ahead of the other riders.

definitions.definition: A silly or credulous person who is prone to mistakes or blunders.
definitions.example: I walked into the wrong bathroom like a total aardvark.

definitions.definition: Of or relating to the Sun, especially rising and setting with the sun.

Of or relating to the rising of a star when it first becomes visible just before sunrise in the eastern sky, having previously been made invisible by sunlight, or the analogous situation of its setting just after sunset.
A British gold coin worth 20 shillings, first produced during the reign of King James I, and bearing a legend indicating the king's intention of uniting the kingdoms of England and Scotland.
To bring together as one.

definitions.definition: To come together as one.

definitions.definition: One who enforces confinement in a jail or prison.
Corpselike; hinting of death; imitating a cadaver.
Of, or relating to the brain, cerebrum, or cerebral cortex.
Intellectual rather than emotional.

definitions.definition: (via gatecrash) To attend a social event without invitation, usually with unfavorable intentions.
definitions.example: We weren't invited to the party so we decided to crash it.

definitions.definition: To accelerate a project or a task or its schedule by devoting more resources to it.

definitions.definition: To give, as a favor.
To lie down for a long rest, sleep or nap, as from tiredness or exhaustion.

To hit or strike with force
